steps.title: Identify the structures around the blank
steps.content: The blank comes after “an approach to music instruction”; the sentence needs a relative clause whose main verb phrase is “emphasizes immersion in a musical community”, with “aiming to teach music in the same natural way children learn language” inserted parenthetically.

steps.title: Apply the rule for relative clauses with inserted description
steps.content:
When a relative clause includes added description, that description must be set off by commas as a parenthetical detail (phrased as “aiming to...” rather than a full verb), so the relative clause maintains one clear main verb.

Choice B — that, aiming correctly uses this structure: “that” beginning the clause, followed by the parenthetical description in commas, then “emphasizes” as the main verb.

The other choices either awkwardly repeat verbs or create a comma splice by attempting two main verbs in the clause.

That matches Choice B — that, aiming.
